div.head: 概述
div.p: 110號窟和109號窟是雙窟，二者窟形相同。該窟内壁刻《大方便佛報恩經》第四卷部分内容和第五卷全部，止於正壁，即e壁中間。

figure.head: 110號窟全景：d壁、e壁、f壁及窟口（自右往左）。2009年拍攝
Panorama view of Cave 110: walls d, e, f and cave entrance (from right to left); photograph taken in 2009

div.head: 位置

list.item: 位於H區，地處水庫一側北邊的崖壁，即107號窟至113號窟這七窟中雙窟的東窟。
f壁方位：145°。

div.p: 窟底距離崖底3.4米左右，高出水位13米左右。此窟與崖壁西側的109號窟由寬0.55米的窟壁隔開，與東側111號窟之間有8米寬的山崖。

div.head: 形制
div.p: 方形敞口窟，長、寬、高均2米餘，尚存雙層窟口的痕迹。窟底大致呈方形，向窟内逐漸擡升。窟頂前端略微隆起，後端平坦。

figure.head: 110號窟平面圖。雷魯卡據馬本漢和王平國的田野考古繪圖製
Ground plan of Cave 110; drawing by Lukas Ledderose after field map by MB and MS

list.item:
d壁：前端高220厘米，後端高206 厘米，
寬196厘米。
e壁：中部高206厘米，寬205厘米。

f壁：前端高216厘米，後端高207厘米，
寬210厘米。
窟口：高214厘米，寬206厘米。

div.head: 保存狀况
div.p: 近窟口的d壁和f壁下端前側脱落，并且破損面延伸至窟内。窟底向窟外延伸約30厘米。與109號窟不同，110號窟前沿處未損毁。
d壁靠近e壁拐角有兩處人爲鑿孔，在刻經區的外側，分别高於地面92－97厘米、115－119厘米。離窟口約80厘米處有一道裂縫，與窟口地面平行，并一直延伸到壁上的兩邊。由全景圖可見這道裂縫（）。

list.item: 打磨區爲194厘米×189厘米，始於窟頂下方6厘米處。窟口起始部分已不可見，止於窟底上方11厘米，距離e壁10－14厘米處。
刻經面爲190厘米×182厘米，始於窟頂下方11－14厘米處，距離窟口約17厘米處的第7行起，刻字可見，止於窟底上方16厘米，距離e壁13－16厘米處。

div.head: 保存狀况
div.p: 保存現狀不一。窟口約15－30厘米處的垂直區域損毁嚴重，爲苔蘚覆蓋。窟底以上約70－80厘米，從窟口處延伸約70厘米的水平區域，漫漶嚴重，刻字所剩無幾。第二段區域狀况類似，在窟底以上約95－102厘米，延伸入窟約50厘米，漫漶嚴重。窟底以上約40－60厘米，在5D區至9D區處，墻壁中央有約52厘米寬的塊狀缺口。地面以上約145厘米的窟口處有一裂縫，9B區與10B區以上126－138厘米，波及經文的第60－70行。地面以上78－87厘米處有一較小的缺口，位於13C區經文的最後4行。除了没有刻經文字的區域以及後端灰白垂直區域，大部分的壁面依然保持原來的淡紅和淡黄的色調。

row.cell: “……尒時……大海留難辛苦……”。爲d壁現存刻字的首行。由於是壁面現存刻字的首行，不是確切的經文起始處，因此加星號*。由於窟口毁損，確切的經文起始處不明。
“大方便［佛］報恩經卷第四”。標識《大方便佛報恩經》第四卷第六品惡友品末。
“大方便佛報恩經慈品第七”。標識《大方便佛報恩經》第五卷第七品慈品的開始。
“……鬼神［人及非人］聞佛法歡喜而［去］復次”。此爲d壁刻經現存最末一行的刻字。

list.item: 打磨區爲189厘米×294厘米，始於距d壁6厘米處，窟頂之下的起始處已不可辨識，終止於窟底以上12厘米，距離f壁10厘米處。這表明此處曾規劃一塊較大的刻經區。
刻經區狹窄，178厘米×105厘米左右，從窟頂下方約10厘米、距離d壁14厘米處開始，終止於窟底以上15厘米，距離f壁89厘米處。

div.head: 保存狀况
div.p: 此窟保存現狀較差。兩處水平區塊的經文缺失，一處位於窟底以上45－70厘米，另一處位於窟底以上74－85厘米，幾乎横跨整個壁面。壁面上端的經文漸次漫漶，大多覆蓋黑緑色苔蘚。

row.cell: “［摩伽陀國有五百群賊……］”。接續d壁最後一行的經文，已不可辨識。
“……在呼噏。尒時五百人中唯有一人……”。爲《大方便佛報恩經》第五卷第七品慈品起始句。
“會大衆聞［法歡喜右遶］而去”。爲倒數第二行現存最後刻字。
“［大方便佛報恩經 卷第五］”。今已不存，爲此窟最後一行，標識《大方便佛報恩經》第五卷第七品慈品的終止。此處據109號窟f壁第69行復原補録。

div.head: 保存狀况
div.p: 窟壁前端覆蓋苔蘚。距窟口邊沿72厘米處有一垂直狹縫，高26厘米、寬2厘米，透過窟壁，延伸至109號窟d壁。整面窟壁覆蓋細密刮痕，平行但大小不一，壁面未經打磨。由此可知刻經的工作程序：工匠在崖壁上鑿出洞窟，留下約2厘米寬的刮痕，如f壁底端所示。隨後刮磨出主區，刮痕更精細，寬0.5厘米左右。這批工匠可能與打磨刻經區的工匠并非同一批人。可以推測，d壁和部分e壁的刊刻完成於f壁打磨工作開始之前。f壁後端的一些部分，其刮痕均漫漶。窟壁上端有黄色和白色痕迹，顔色稍亮，而較低處爲較暗的黑色和褐色調。

div.head: 討論
div.bibl:
彭家勝第8頁
作者基於胡文和的研究，測量該窟體積爲10.92立方米。作者辨識刻經爲《大方便佛報恩經》，認爲d壁刊刻第七品和第八品，兩品皆屬“慈品”。作者指出，e壁和f壁刻經無法辨識。

胡文和第10頁
胡文和提供的該窟測量數據與彭家勝所提供相同。作者重申d壁刊刻第七品和第八品爲“慈品”，未提及e壁和f壁。

胡文和第69頁
胡文和的看法同其此前研究。

李良和鄧之金第45頁
李良和鄧之金給出了此窟及每壁刻經區的測量數據，統計了刻經總字數。作者認爲，d壁刊刻《大方便佛報恩經》第七品和第八品，并指出f壁未刻經文。

《成都考古發現》第397、408頁
成都市文物考古研究所提供了此窟測量數據，以及詳細的描述。作者認爲，第六品“惡友品”和第七品“慈品”刻於d壁。e壁和f壁已打磨，但尚未刻經。作者對比《大正藏》本把d壁《大方便佛報恩經》第七品和第八品刻經行數排表列出。

李琛妍第41、50－51、53頁，以及第76頁，注58
見本卷109號窟的討論。

曹丹和于春第14－15頁
曹丹和于春提供了此窟壁面的測量數據，并依據拓片推測刻經總字數：d壁9020字，e壁1275字。作者辨識d壁刻經爲《大方便佛報恩經》第七品“慈品”；因爲壁面風化不清，未確認e壁刻經内容。

秦臻、張雪芬和雷玉華第38－42、126、471－481頁
作者引用《成都考古發現》。此外，作者提供d壁刻經位置示意圖，并整理此壁刻經録文，按壁面細節加以分析。e壁刻經未收録。

肥田路美第111－112頁
肥田路美依據d壁刻經的拓片，辨識《大方便佛報恩經》第六品和第七品。因風化受損，e壁和f壁刻經無法辨識，而肥田路美推測此經全文刊刻於窟壁，直到第七卷第九品終止。

鄧星亮第81頁和第94頁
鄧星亮提供此窟的測量尺寸，并且大致描述基本情况。作者認爲經文刻於d壁和e壁。作者辨識d壁刻經爲《大方便佛報恩經》第五卷第七品，并在e壁繼續刊刻此品。作者轉録了可辨認部分的開篇與結尾。

div.head: Overview
div.p: Cave 110 is the twin to Cave 109 and is of the same standard size. It contains part of scroll 4 and the entire scroll 5 of the Repaying Kindness Sutra. The engraving stopped in the middle of the rear wall e.

div.head: Location

list.item: Section H, in the northern cliff beyond the water reservoir. The eastern of the two sutra caves in a row of seven Caves (107–113).
Direction of wall f: 145˚.

div.p: The floor lies ca. 3.4 m above the foot of the cliff, and ca. 13 m above water level. The cave is separated from Cave 109 to the west by a wall, 0.55 m in width, and from Cave 111 to the east by an eight-meter-wide cliff section.

div.head: Shape
div.p: Box shaped with open front; standard box size with height, depth, and width, all slightly over 2 m; remnants of a double entrance frame; an approximately square ground plan rises towards the rear; the ceiling bulges slightly in the front part, it is flat in the rear.

list.item:
Wall d: height at the front 220 cm,
at the rear 206 cm, width 196 cm.

Wall e: height at midpoint 206 cm,
width 205 cm.
Wall f: height at the front 216 cm, at the rear 207 cm, width 210 cm.
Entrance: height 214 cm, width 206 cm.

figure.head: 110號窟平面圖。雷魯卡據馬本漢和王平國田野繪圖製
Ground plan of Cave 110; drawing by Lukas Ledderose after field map by MB and MS

div.head: State of Presentation
div.p: At the entrance, the lower parts of walls d and f have lost their straight front sides; they recede inwards and are worn. The floor extends ca. 30 cm outwards from the cave; its front edge is not broken, unlike that of its twin Cave 109.

In the rear of the wall d, near the corner with wall e, and outside the field of script are two round man-made holes, at a height of 92–97 cm, and 115–119 cm. Ca. 80 cm back from the front edge, a crack runs parallel to the entrance over the floor and continues into both walls. The crack is visible on the panorama view (fig. ).

list.item: A polished area, 194 × 189 cm, begins 6 cm below the ceiling; the beginning at the entrance is not visible. It ends 10–14 cm from wall e and 11 cm above the floor.

The field of script, 190 × 182 cm, begins 11–14 cm below the ceiling, ca. 17 cm from the entrance the first column, no. 7, becomes visible. The field of script ends 16 cm above the floor and 13–16 cm before wall e.

div.head: State of Preservation
div.p: Mixed. A ca. 15–30 cm wide vertical zone at the entrance is badly worn and covered by moss. Almost no characters are left in a horizontal zone, ca. 70–80 cm above the floor, and extending from the entrance ca. 70 cm into the cave; and in a second zone, ca. 95–102 cm above the floor, and extending ca. 50 cm into the cave. At a height of ca. 40–60 cm above the floor, a 52 cm wide flaked patch in the center of the wall runs through segments 5D–9D. A crack, beginning near the entrance ca. 145 cm above the floor, widens into a hole, 126–138 cm above the ground, affecting columns 60–70 in segments 9B and 10B. A smaller patch 78–87 cm above the floor cuts into the last four columns in segment 13C. Except for the zones devoid of characters and a grayish vertical zone at the rear, the surface in most parts preserves its original color in fresh reddish and yellowish tinges.

row.cell: ……尒時……大海留難辛苦……; first visible characters on this wall.This is the first visible column of the wall, not the exact beginning of the wall. Thus * is added. Since the entrance of the cave is damaged, the exact beginning cannot be determined.
大方便[佛]報恩經卷第四; the end of scroll 4, coinciding with the end of chapter 6, entitled Eyou pin 惡友品, of the Repaying Kindness Sutra.
大方便佛報恩經慈品第七; the beginning of chapter 7, entitled Ci pin 慈品, coinciding with the beginning of scroll 5 of the Repaying Kindness Sutra.
……鬼神[人及非人]聞佛法歡喜而[去]復次; the last visible characters on wall d.

list.item: A polished area, 189 × 294 cm, begins 6 cm from wall d, the beginning below the ceiling is not visible; it ends 10 cm from wall f and 12 cm above the floor, indicating that a wider field of script was planned.
A narrow field of script, ca. 178 × 105 cm, begins 14 cm from wall d, and ca. 10 cm below the ceiling; it ends 15 cm above the floor and 89 cm from wall f.

div.head: State of Preservation
div.p: Poor. Two horizontal zones, with characters lacking, one 45–70 cm and one 74–85 cm above the floor, run over almost the entire width of the wall. In the upper part of the wall the characters gradually become fainter. Large parts of the wall are covered with greenish and black lichen.

row.cell: [摩伽陀國有五百群賊……]; continuing from the last column in wall d, no longer visible.
在呼噏。尒時五百人中唯有一人是……; the first visible characters in chapter 7, entitled Ci pin 慈品, in scroll 5 of the Repaying Kindness Sutra.
會大衆聞[法歡喜右遶]而去; the last visible characters on this wall in the column next to the last.

[大方便佛報恩經 卷第五]; no longer visible; the last column in this cave; the end of scroll 5, coinciding with the end of chapter 7, entitled Ci pin 慈品, in the Repaying Kindness Sutra. Reconstruction by analogy with Cave 109, wall f, column 69.

div.head: State of Preservation
div.p: The front part of the wall is covered with moss. 72 cm from the front edge of the floor, a vertical crack, 26 cm high and 2 cm wide, goes through the wall into wall d of Cave 109. The entire wall is finely scraped with parallel striations across irregularly shaped areas, but no area has been polished. This again allows for a glimpse into the working process. The workmen who carved the cave out of the rock left striations ca. 2 cm wide, as seen on the base of wall f. Then they scraped the main wall with finer striations, ca. 0.5 cm wide. These workers may not have been the ones who prepared the polished field or the field of script. It is conceivable that the engraving on wall d and on part of wall e was completed before polishing work had begun on wall f. In some areas of the rear part of wall f, all striations have flaked away. In the upper part of the wall, the color is lighter, with yellowish and whitish tinges, whereas in the lower part darker, blackish and brownish tones prevail.

note.p: Peng (based on Hu Wenhe’s work) gives measurements of the cave and calculates its volume at 10.92 cubic meters. He identifies the Repaying Kindness Sutra and says that wall d includes the seventh and eighth chapters, both entitled Chapter on Compassion (Ci pin 慈品). He writes that it is unclear which texts were carved on walls e and f.

Hu Wenhe gives the measurements of the cave, following . He repeats Peng’s identification of the Chapter on Compassion (Ci pin
慈品) on wall d, which he numbers as chapters 7 and 8. He does not mention walls e and f.
Hu Wenhe repeats the information in his earlier publication, .

The authors give measurements for the cave and for the fields of script on each wall, also calculating the total number of carved characters. They note that the Repaying Kindness Sutra’s seventh and eighth chapters are carved on wall d. They say that no text was carved on wall f.

The editors of the Chengdu Municipal Institute of Cultural Relics and Archaeology give measurements and a detailed description of the cave. They write that the sixth Chapter on Bad Friend (Eyou pin 惡友品) and the seventh Chapter on Compassion (Ci pin
慈品) were carved on wall d. Albeit walls e and f were polished, no texts were carved. They tabulate the column numbers for both chapters of the Repaying Kindness Sutra carved on wall d, with reference to the Taishō edition.

Cao and Yu give measurements of the walls, their surface areas, and, based on rubbings, the number of extant characters: wall d, 9,020; wall e, 1,275. They identify the seventh Chapter on Compassion (Ci pin 慈品) of the Repaying Kindness Sutra on wall d, yet they cannot identify the text carved on wall e due to erosion.

The authors repeat the information in . In addition, they provide a diagrams of wall d and transcribe the engraved text on this wall, which they analyze according to their own detailed scheme. They do not transcribe the text on wall e.

On the basis of the rubbing of the text carved on wall d, Hida identifies the sixth and seventh chapter of the Repaying Kindness Sutra. Due to erosion, the texts carved on wall e and f cannot be deciphered, yet Hida suggests that the sutra was completly carved, until the end of chapter 9 in the seventh scroll.

Deng Xingliang gives measurements and a general description of the cave. He notes that sutra texts were carved on walls d and e. He identifies chapter 7 from the fifth scroll of the Repaying Kindness Sutra on wall d, and the continuation of this chapter on wall e. He transcribes the beginning and the end of the legible passages.
